interactions.summary: Jiang extends the post-globalization trends into regional examples: Greater Israel in the Middle East, East Asian resource competition, and a potentially aggressive remilitarized Japan, then summarizes the three trends again.
Danny asks how Jiang sees the Greater Israel project developing when Iran is not collapsing and Israel still faces Hezbollah, Iraq, Yemen, and shipping-route pressures.

Jiang answers through theology: Israeli religious extremists can see war, U.S. exit, GCC destruction, Saudi/Turkish expansion, and even pain inflicted on secular Tel Aviv as steps toward Greater Israel and a more theocratic Israel.

speaker_notes.note: Danny's Greater Israel question continues across two segments.

claims.claim: Jiang predicts regional mercantilism may include Israel pursuing Greater Israel to control trade through the Mediterranean, Middle East, and Africa, while East Asian states compete for independent supply chains and resources.
claims.temporal_scope: Prediction for the next months and years after the Iran war.

claims.claim: Jiang says Israel's religious self-understanding matters because some Israeli extremists interpret the war as following God's plan and advancing Greater Israel from the Nile to the Euphrates.
claims.temporal_scope: Interpretation of Israeli war aims on 2026-03-13.

claims.claim: In Jiang's reading, an Israeli extremist objective is not simply defeating Iran but using the war to expel America from the Middle East, destroy Saudi Arabia, draw Turkey in, and transform Israel itself toward theocracy.
claims.temporal_scope: Interpretive model of war aims as of 2026-03-13.

claims.claim: Jiang distinguishes Tel Aviv and Jerusalem as opposing Israeli centers of gravity: secular, Western, cosmopolitan Israel versus inward-looking religious theocracy.
claims.temporal_scope: Model of Israeli internal politics applied to the current war.

signature_moments.moment: Tel Aviv and Jerusalem become two rival souls inside Israel.
signature_moments.source_phrase: two opposing centers of gravity in Israel
signature_moments.why_it_matters: The phrase gives the reader a concrete internal map of Jiang's Israel theology argument.

signature_moments.moment: In Jiang's account of extremist theology, destruction becomes a route to repentance.
signature_moments.source_phrase: redemption is only possible through pain, suffering
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This preserves the disturbing moral mechanism Jiang attributes to religious extremists.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: Jiang's term for an Israeli religious-geopolitical project extending from the Nile to the Euphrates and using regional war to remake power in the Middle East.
A symbolic split between secular, Western, democratic Israel and religious, conservative, theocratic Israel.

chronology_notes.note: Jiang says some of these regional consequences could unfold over months, while also presenting Greater Israel as a long religious project; keep short-term war prediction separate from long-horizon theology.

uncertainty_notes.note: The transcript has an ASR interruption around 'sorry that was a mistake'; the usable claim is the Tel Aviv/Jerusalem political-theological contrast.
